query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "mint: managed infrastructure for training and serving millions of llms" computer science
rationale: This paper addresses the "Training" component of the user's question regarding "Managed Infrastructure for Training and Serving Millions of LLMs" by proposing a novel framework (HDS) for optimizing data scheduling and mixing strategies during LLM pre-training. It satisfies acceptance criteria (a) and (b) as it measures the same mechanism (data composition and training efficiency) and variables (perplexity, training iterations) within the same domain (LLM infrastructure and training pipelines).
